ain: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Ainu.

akin: adjective. Of persons, of the same kin; related by blood.
ani: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Andi.
ark: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikapú.

car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
cark: verb. To be filled with worry, solicitude, or troubles.

cigar: noun. A cylinder of tobacco rolled and wrapped with an outer covering of tobacco leaves, intended to be smoked.
cracking: noun. The thermal decomposition of a substance, especially that of crude petroleum in order to produce petrol / gasoline.
crag: noun. A rocky outcrop; a rugged steep cliff or rock.
crick: noun. A painful stiffness in some part of the body, like the neck or back, which makes it difficult to move the affected body part for some
gan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gan.
gar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Galeya.
grin: noun. A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
ink: noun. A pigment (or dye)-based fluid used for writing, printing etc.
irk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Iraqw.
kiang: noun. A large wild ass, Equus kiang, native to the Tibetan Plateau.
kin: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kinyarwanda.
kina: noun. The national currency of Papua New Guinea, divided into 100 toea.
king: noun. A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy; in an absolute monarchy, the supreme ruler of his nation.
kir: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kyrgyz.
knar: noun. A knot or burl in a tree; a knurl, a gnarl.

rain: noun. Condensed water falling from a cloud.

rani: noun. The wife of a rajah.

rick: noun. Straw, hay etc. stored in a stack for winter fodder, commonly protected with thatch.

rin: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nungu.
ring: noun. A solid object in the shape of a circle.
